author_identification.canonical_name_ar: أبو عبد الله محمد بن أبي محمد السقطي المالقي الأندلسي
author_identification.scholarly_standing: Andalusi Maliki scholar known for his work on market inspection and public morality.

scholarly_context.tradition_position: A practical guide on the role and duties of the market inspector (muhtasib) within the Maliki legal tradition.
scholarly_context.historical_significance: One of the notable works on hisba (market inspection) from the Islamic West, reflecting Andalusi concerns with public morality and economic fairness.
scholarly_context.muhaqiq_reputation: Levi-Provençal and Collin are respected early 20th-century Orientalist editors, though their philological methods may not meet contemporary standards.
scholarly_context.tahqiq_methodology_note: Early 20th-century European academic edition, likely relying on available manuscripts without extensive collation.
